retry_reason: The operation is unconditional and always proceeds; failures in __bpf_obj_drop_impl are not meaningful for retry as the tree has already been emptied.
contract_summary: Bulk-release wrapper for a BPF rbtree: atomically replaces the root with an empty tree under a spinlock, then iterates over all original nodes and drops each object's reference via __bpf_obj_drop_impl. This releases all references held by the tree, potentially freeing objects if their refcount reaches zero. The caller must ensure no concurrent access to the tree after this call. The spinlock is acquired and released internally; no external lock is required. After this call, the tree is empty and can be reused.

judge.reason: The sole purpose is to drop all objects stored in an rbtree by calling __bpf_obj_drop_impl (a put) on each node. No other business logic exists beyond iteration and put.
